title: Lineum: A Continuous Emergent Simulation (reference implementation & evidence bundle)
description:
Lineum is a deterministic, continuous simulation governed by a coupled non-linear partial differential equation (PDE) system. This simulation demonstrates how complex, physics-like phenomena—including dynamic quasiparticle formation, interference patterns, orbital mechanics, and apparent gravitational attraction—can spontaneously emerge from purely local, low-level interactions without the imposition of any predefined spatial metric or fundamental forces.

This repository provides the reference implementation of the Lineum computational engine, alongside canonical HTML reports and visual artifacts (CSV/PNG/GIF). The core methodological principles, including the definition of the central variables, SI conversions (e.g., energy, wavelength, and scale-relative effective mass constraints), and the criteria for Structural Closure governed by center-trace half-life dynamics, are rigorously documented in the accompanying core whitepaper (whitepaper/01-core-principles.md). The operational evidence and falsifiable audits (C1/C2/C3 checks) are located in the output/ directory, providing a robust, commit-anchored foundation for independent reproducibility.
notes: The core methodology is detailed in `whitepaper/01-core-principles.md` (manuscript text and figures are provided under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0). All software and accompanying scripts are released under the AGPL-3.0 license. Please note: The display-only effective mass is provided strictly as a unit conversion derived from the canonical tone f0 (m = h f0 / c^2, using m/m_e as a scale reference) and does not constitute a rest-mass claim. The property of Structural Closure is defined within the scope of the v1.0.x core as an operational consequence of the φ center-trace half-life metric. Advanced phenomena, such as the Return Echo or Dimensional Transparency (time-varying κ dynamics), are the subject of ongoing research tracks (lineum-exp-*, lineum-extension-*) and fall outside the scope of this foundational core bundle. Acknowledgements: Kateřina Marečková provided critical opposition and falsification testing. Vlastimil Smeták contributed structural insights correlating with his OEA model.
